name: Quay Schools
description_short: Quay Schools Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit credit union established in 1961, serving educational institution members in Tucumcari, NM with $8M+ in assets.
description_long:
Quay Schools Federal Credit Union was established in 1961 and is headquartered in Tucumcari, New Mexico. As a federally-chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative. The institution specializes in serving the educational sector, catering specifically to students, faculty, and alumni of educational institutions with tailored banking solutions.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), personal and auto loans, wire transfers, online bill pay, and mobile banking. They provide competitive lending rates across multiple loan categories: new vehicle loans at 6.04% APR, used vehicle loans at 8.22% APR, and other unsecured lines of credit at 12.02% APR. Members can access financial calculators, interest rate comparisons, and 24/7 online banking and mobile app services.

Quay Schools distinguishes itself through its not-for-profit structure, which allows it to offer better interest rates and lower fees compared to traditional for-profit banks. The organization maintains strong community ties with over 1,100 loyal members and approximately 4 dedicated employees. Their membership specialization in educational communities creates a focused member base with aligned financial needs and values.

The primary limitation is geographic and membership-based accessibility—the credit union operates with only 1 physical branch in Tucumcari with no ATM network, potentially constraining convenience for members outside the immediate area. Membership is restricted to individuals affiliated with educational institutions, which narrows their customer base. Additionally, there are currently no member reviews available on the platform, making it difficult to assess real-world service quality and member satisfaction.
services: Financial Calculators and Interest Rate Comparisons
pros: Not-for-profit structure delivers competitive rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Specialized focus on educational institution members with tailored financial solutions
Strong asset base of $8M+ and stable 60+ year operating history since 1961
24/7 online banking and mobile app access available for iOS and Android
Competitive auto loan rates (6.04% new, 8.22% used) and multiple lending products
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns institutional interests with member benefits
Online bill pay, wire transfers, and financial calculators available to members
cons: Only 1 physical branch location in Tucumcari with 0 ATMs limits accessibility and convenience
Restricted membership eligibility—only serves educational institution affiliates, not the general public
Small member base (1,100+) and minimal employee count (4) may limit service depth and availability
No member reviews available on platform makes it difficult to verify service quality claims
Limited geographic footprint and branch network compared to larger regional or national credit unions
best_for: Students, faculty, and alumni of educational institutions seeking member-focused banking
Educational sector employees wanting lower fees and competitive rates through a not-for-profit cooperative
Individuals in or near Tucumcari, NM seeking personalized community banking relationships
Members prioritizing institution alignment with educational values and community-oriented finance
diagnosis: Quay Schools Federal Credit Union is best for educational institution affiliates in or near Tucumcari, NM seeking competitive rates and personalized service through a not-for-profit cooperative. The primary caveat is limited geographic accessibility (1 branch, no ATMs) and restricted membership eligibility that excludes general public applicants.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
